titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Willis Photographs of Arizona, Connecticut, New Mexico, and South Dakota
Willis (John) Photographs of Arizona, Connecticut, New Mexico, and South Dakota
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 17, 2021 at 03:03

did.unittitle: Photographs of Arizona, Connecticut, New Mexico, and South Dakota

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Inkjet prints of photographs by John Willis that document sites, events, and persons in Arizona, Connecticut, New Mexico, and South Dakota in 2010-2011.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints of photographs by John Willis that document sites, events, and persons in Arizona, Connecticut, New Mexico, and South Dakota in 2010-2011.

Photographs of Arizona include views of Canyon de Chelly and Winslow as well as communities at Navajo Nation including Chinle, Kayenta, and Tuba City, as well as discrete groups of images in Window Rock of the Navajo Military Graveyard and "wheatpaste" photographic poster murals by physician photographer James Edward Thomas, also known as Chip Thomas or "jetsonorama." Other images include views of the Navajo Generating Station, a coal-fired power plant located near Page.
A single view in Connecticut documents a billboard near Waterbury.

Photographs of New Mexico include views of El Santuario de Chimayó in Chimayo, Edgewood, El Morro National Monument, Penitentiary of New Mexico, Silver City, Truth or Consequences, and Zuni Indian Reservation.
Photographs of South Dakota document sites at Interior as well as documenting the town's Interior Frontier Days, a rodeo held on Independence Day weekend.
The inkjet prints in this series measure 28.8 x 43.2 centimeters on paper of various sizes approximately 33 x 48 centimeters.
Signed by photographer and manuscript titles on verso of most prints.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Willis on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Arizona, 2010-2011, II. New Mexico, 2011, III. South Dakota, 2011.

bioghist.head: John Willis (born 1957)
bioghist.p: John Willis is a documentary photographer and instructor of photography at Marlboro College in Marlboro, Vermont. His monographs include Recycled Realities , co-authored with Tom Young (Center for American Places, 2002) and Views from the Reservation (Center for American Places, 2010). Willis has photographs in the permanent collections of the Whitney Museum of American Art, the Museum of Fine Arts in Boston, and the Museum of Fine Arts in Houston, among others. He is also a co-founder with Bill Ledger of the In-Sight Photography Project, a photography outreach project to youth in Brattleboro, Vermont.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Willis, Photographs of Arizona, Connecticut, New Mexico, and South Dakota. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

subject.part: Navajo Nation, Arizona, New Mexico & Utah
Navajo Nation, Arizona, New Mexico & Utah

geogname.part: Navajo Nation, Arizona, New Mexico & Utah

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into six subseries: Canyon de Chelly, Chinle, Navajo Indian Reservation, Navajo Generating Station, Navajo Veterans' Cemetery, and Winslow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits made at a senior center in Tsidi To'ii (known as Blue Springs before 2012), which is a Chapter, a semi-self autonomous local government, in the Navajo Nation, Arizona, New Mexico & Utah.

title.part: Jetsonorama, Navajo Graffiti Artist's Work on Site
Little Miss and Mr. Singer, Little Singer Community School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Navajo Generating Station is a coal-fired powerplant located on the Navajo Nation, near Page, Arizona.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly Detail views of weathered United States flags at Navajo Veterans' Cemetery at Window Rock, Arizona.

title.part: Navajo Veteran's Flag #3, Navajo Military Graveyard

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into eight subseries: Chimayo, Edgewood, El Morro National Monument, Penitentiary of New Mexico, Silver City, Truth or Consequences, Zuni Indian Reservation, and Unidentified Sites.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Willis identifies the locality as Esperanza, New Mexico.

title.part: The Wall Petroglyphs from Pueblo People and Carvings from Spanish Explorers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Detail of carved inscriotions on El Morro, a sandstone promontory also known as A'ts'ina (Zuni for "place of writings on the rock") and Inscription Rock.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The New Mexico State Penitentiary is a men's maximum-security prison located in unincorporated Santa Fe County. In 1980, Cell Block 4 of the prison was the scene of one of the most violent prison riots in the correctional history of the United States. This section of the prison was closed in 1998 and is now referred to as the "Old Main."

title.part: Roadside Altar by the Rio Grande

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is organized into two subseries: Interior and Interior Frontier Days.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rodeo held on Independence Day weekend at the Interior Frontier Days Rodeo Grounds in Interior, South Dakota. Identified by Willis as "Freedom Days, 4th of July Rodeo."
